narratives.full_description:
A concert and film screening featuring unpublished images shot in Bourj Hammoud (Beirut) presented by Roberto Paci Dalò

Addressing the tragedy of the Armenian Genocide, the artist creates a composition where texts by Daniel Varoujan (Հացին Երգը / The Song of the Bread) intertwine with a soundscape of electronic textures, acoustic instruments, and voices.

1915 The Armenian Files & Conspiratio 
 1915 The Armenian Files & Conspiratio 
 Concert by Roberto Paci Daló 
 
 This is a unique occasion to experience Roberto Paci Daló's work live — a concert and film screening featuring unpublished images shot in Bourj Hammoud (Beirut). 
 
 Addressing the tragedy of the Armenian Genocide, the artist creates a composition where texts by Daniel Varoujan (Հացին Երգը / The Song of the Bread) intertwine with a soundscape of electronic textures, acoustic instruments, and voices. 
 
 This performance marks a significant milestone: despite his long-standing artistic research into Armenian culture, this is the artist's first time presenting his work here. 
 
 Location : Martiros Sarian House-Museum (3 Martiros Saryan St) 
 Date : 27 November 19:00 
 Language : English 
 
 
 Partners 
 Partners 
 
 Roberto Paci Dalò 
 
 Roberto Paci Dalò is a composer, director of Giardini Pensili, and a pioneer in the use of new technologies in the arts. His work has been supported by figures such as John Cage, Aleksandr Sokurov, and John Zorn.
